free_text: Study of the reactions T(p,n)3He,7Li(p,n)7Be,
9Be(p,n)9B and 19F(p,n)19Ne
Counter ratio technique was used (see REL-REF).
- Counter ratio
technique description.
Data for reactions T(p,n)3He and 7Li(p,n)7Be are given
in counter ratio units.
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Subents
004-007 added. SAMPLE moved to Subents 002-005.
BIB updated.
An evaporated beryllium target, about 8 keV thick at a
proton energy of 2 MeV, was used in the experiment.
The counter ratio and the yield of neutrons in the
forward direction were measured for proton energies
from threshold to 5.8 MeV.
A study was done with the counter ratio technique the
low-lying level structure of the residual nuclei.
Fig 4 Phys.Rev.,100(1955)91
Value used for normalization.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG. ERR-ANALYS
deleted. STATUS updated. FLAG deleted (Value moved to
Subent 004).
An evaporated A1F3 target, which was about 7 keV thick
at a proton energy of 4.5 MeV, was used in the
experiment. The counter ratio and the forward yield of
neutrons were measured from threshold to 5.9 MeV.
A study was done with the counter ratio technique the
low-lying level structure of the residual nuclei.
Fig 5 Phys.Rev.,100(1955)91
Value used for normalization.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG. ERR-ANALYS
deleted. STATUS updated. FLAG deleted (Value moved to
Subent 005).
A 9Be target of 171 mu-g/cm2
The cross sections were determined by comparing
the counting rates in a long counter from the proton
bombardment of these targets with that from a weighted
LiF target, since the cross section for the reaction
7Li(p,n) is well known.
Value from the text on p.96 of
